item.title: US secures rare earths supply as part of $565mn loan to Brazil mining group
item.description: Investment chief at US International Development Finance Corporation says agreement includes offtake controls

item.title: France probes Iran links in thwarted attack on Bank of America
item.description: Police arrest three men in possible connection with new Iranian group Ashab al-Yamin

item.title: Chelsea FC posts record Premier League loss
item.description: Losses by football club owned by Todd Boehly and Clearlake come despite revenues increasing to £491mn

item.title: Intel strikes $14bn deal with Apollo to reclaim Irish chip plant
item.description: Chipmaker sold stake two years ago to help shore up its precarious finances

item.title: UAE curbs entry by Iranian nationals
item.description: Gulf state imposes restrictions after facing attacks from Islamic republic

item.title: Mooted Pernod-Jack Daniel’s merger should age well
item.description: Given their fall in shares and a general decline in drinking, a get-together makes sense

item.title: Nike shares drop on unexpected forecast for sales decline
item.description: Sportswear group’s softer than anticipated outlook stirs concerns about multiyear turnaround plan

item.title: UK borrowers coming off five-year fixes face biggest mortgage ‘shock’
item.description: Those refinancing a five-year fix face paying an extra £395 a month on average — but will still be paying less than those who fixed two years ago

item.title: Does it really make sense to retrain as a plumber?
item.description: It’s become the go-to advice for anyone whose white-collar job could be eliminated by AI — but it’s not very realistic

item.title: US investors prefer Europemaxxing to Europebashing
item.description: Venture capital firms know their dollars go a lot further here than in Silicon Valley

item.title: Europe must pay more for medicines, says Bayer
item.description: German group’s pharma business expected to generate most of its revenues from US after strategic shift

item.title: The growing crackdown on corporate child exploitation
item.description: A golden age for lucrative kids’ marketing is coming to an overdue end

item.title: A spokesperson could not be reached for comment (possibly because she doesn’t exist)
item.description: The curious case of Emilia Carrière, alleged comms professional

item.title: Berkeley shares tumble as housebuilder halts land purchases
item.description: UK group blames Middle East conflict and deteriorating outlook for investment cuts

item.title: Airlines in crisis mode as Iran war hits jet fuel supplies
item.description: Carriers scale back expansion plans as oil prices soar

item.title: BP’s new chief promises ‘clear direction’ after turbulent year
item.description: Meg O’Neill’s first message to staff pledges improved performance in difficult geopolitical environment

item.title: EU warns of prolonged crisis as Iran war adds €14bn to energy import bill
item.description: Also in this newsletter: US criticises ‘unfortunate’ conditions added to EU trade deal

item.title: FirstFT: US could end Iran war within ‘2 or 3 weeks’, Trump says
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: dealmaking’s blockbuster quarter and German nuclear power rethink

item.title: European fiscal support for high energy prices still targeted and temporary
item.description: Central banks in the region have difficult trade-offs to make and government action generally complicates things

item.title: The great unwinding of Unilever
item.description: The FTSE 100 group is combining its food business with McCormick to create a $66bn food giant

item.title: German economy minister urges nuclear power rethink as energy prices soar
item.description: Katherina Reiche’s call comes as Berlin launches investment summit to help revive Europe’s largest economy

item.title: High street banks could learn from Revolut’s fee bonanza
item.description: Higher account fees would reduce exposure to interest rates for old-school lenders

item.title: California’s fuel needs ‘left in the lurch’ by Iran war
item.description: Most populous US state is confronting tight supplies of petrol and jet fuel from Asia

item.title: ‘Desperate’ Asia turns to Russian oil amid Iran energy shock
item.description: Import-dependent countries take advantage of US sanctions waiver to replace disrupted Gulf supply

item.title: Lessons from your petrol pump
item.description: As consumers, sharp price increases are unwanted. But they send an essential signal

item.title: Ploughshares to swords: a call to arms for industrial Europe
item.description: Carmakers get another chance to revolutionise their production lines amid rearmament push

item.title: Oaktree’s path puts private credit choices into relief
item.description: Market stress to test whether it is right not to pursue scale as aggressively and broadly as some peers

item.title: Can I get money back from my ex-tenants?
item.description: They have taken back their deposit by hacking into the letting agent’s deposit account

item.title: Record number of megadeals agreed in first quarter
item.description: Twenty-two transactions each valued above $10bn announced in past three months

item.title: Coffee chain Blank Street in talks to raise more than $100mn in fresh funding
item.description: Company known for its matcha drinks has rapidly expanded
